IBEW.text:
**Trampin the IBEW Indoor Locals — Monday, July 13, 2026**

**LU-481 Indianapolis, IN** is running hot. Meade Electric's Meta Data Center gig pays **$53.80/hr + 10% overscale** on a brutal 5-12s Mon-Fri plus 10-hour Saturdays—that's real money if you can stack the hours. Ermco's Google Data Center in Mooresville is cleaner at 5-10s with one Saturday. Book depth sits around 191 hands for 43 calls; first in line should be moving fast.

**LU-1426 Grand Forks, ND** is the dark horse. Parsons Electric's Polaris Forge Data Center pays **$44.80/hr base + $100/day non-taxed per diem** (if you're 100+ miles out) on a 6-10 schedule with another **$100 weekday/$150 weekend incentive**—that's nearly $70/day in the pocket before OT. Thirty-five and 24 openings respectively. Hunt Electric's Grand Forks Air Base gig is more reasonable at 4-10s.

**LU-430 Racine, WI** is locked in on Microsoft Mount Pleasant at **$62.51/hr**. Gerdes, Pieper, Staff Electric, and Kelso Burnett all feeding the same project; 53 total calls, book under 76 hands. WI inside JW card or reciprocal required, but if you've got it, this is top-tier Midwest scale with zero travel headache.

**LU-613 Atlanta, GA** spreads 39 calls across Inglett & Stubbs (QTS Fayette, Wellstar Paulding Hospital), Miller Electrical (cable splicer work), and Beltline Electric—all paying **$40–$42.18/hr** on 4-10 or 5-10 schedules. Book depth 232 hands; solid volume, moderate pay.

**LU-583 El Paso, TX** is slinging **$55/hr with all OT double time** across Summit Line, Rosendin, and Cupertino. Thin board (10 calls, 114 hands) but high velocity. Requires NM State JIW License EE98J.

**Market snapshot:** Data centers are the spine—Port Washington, Polaris Forge, Meta, Google all burning through crews on 6-day schedules and heavy OT. Eastern boards (Newark, DC, Charlotte) are shifting toward night-shift premiums and boot vouchers; the work's there, but it's graveyard push.

LINEMAN.text:
**Trampin the IBEW Lineman Locals — Monday, July 13, 2026**

**LU-1426 Grand Forks, ND** is running hot with 25 calls and only ~143 hands on the board—that's work-heavy ratio. Parsons Electric's got **$44.80/hr** inside wireman slots on the Polaris Forge Data Center (Harwood), but the real money is the **$100/day non-taxed per diem plus $100–$150 weekend incentive** stacked on 6-10s. Hunt Electric's also hiring 10 IW at the Grand Forks Air Base, same rate, 4-10s with OT Fridays. Data centers are printing work in the upper Midwest right now.

**LU-71 Columbus, OH** has 34 calls chasing 109 hands—prime distribution work. New River Electric Corp. is running **$61.44/hr journeyman lineman** jobs across central and southern Ohio distro, **$125 night per diem, $100 bonus**, 4-10 schedules. CDL A required. JJM Power's got smaller crews (4 man) on Chillicothe work, same rate, **$100/day per diem**. Thayer's make-ready and reconduct calls sweeten the pool. Heavy demand, tight board.

**LU-1547 Anchorage, AK** is starving—39 calls but only 59 hands available, yet most postings are garbage: temp labor at **$30.86/hr**, maintenance worker slots at **$21–$24/hr**. Skip unless you're stuck. The telecom I&R work via Alaska Communications hits **$45.31** and was posted in April, so likely filled.

**LU-583 El Paso, TX** has solid **$55.00/hr top scale** lineman work through Summit and Cupertino Electric, but all jobs mandate **NM State JIW License EE98J**—gatekeep issue. Rosendin's offering **$40 + $15 incentive** for hitting 90% of 60/hr/week. All overtime double time.

**LU-474 Memphis, TN** and **LU-194 Shreveport, LA** are both slinging data-center and fiber jobs at **$36.65–$36.65/hr** with **6-10s or 5-10s schedules**, heavy weekend OT. Thin margins but steady calls.

**Data centers and fiber backbone projects are vacuuming up linemen faster than traditional utility work—check your licensing reqs before you roll.**
